title: Persistent goals move coding agents beyond single sessions.
summary: Codex /goal gives the agent a persistent objective, not just more chat history. It points coding agents toward longer arcs of work where staying oriented matters as much as writing code.
operator_consequence: Developers now need to know which persistent goal shaped a run, whether it still matches the project direction, and how it relates to the current task.

title: Agent memory is becoming a product surface.
summary: Claude recaps, Gemini memory patches, Hermes Curator, and resume state all show that agent tools are learning how to carry context forward.
operator_consequence: Developers should use agent-side memory, but should preserve a project-owned record of what context actually shaped serious work.

title: Permissions are getting clearer, but every agent does them differently.
summary: Agent tools increasingly expose permission, trust, environment, hook, MCP, extension, and transport settings, but each uses its own vocabulary.
operator_consequence: Developers need to know what the agent could read, what it could change, which plugins were enabled, which credentials were exposed, and which sandbox was active.

title: Review is moving inside the agent tools.
summary: Claude cloud review, Codex multi-agent controls, Gemini eval work, and Hermes Curator reports all point toward agent tools that can critique their own work.
operator_consequence: Developers should treat native agent review as useful evidence, not final proof that the project is correct.

title: Plugins and skills are becoming the new agent interface.
summary: The practical power of agent CLIs is moving into plugins, hooks, extensions, skills, and transport modules.
operator_consequence: Developers should inspect plugin, extension, skill, hook, and transport surfaces before trusting serious run behavior.

title: Do not build your workflow around one agent's current integration list.
summary: Provider lists, release channels, app-server surfaces, and built-in integrations change quickly enough that wrappers must stay thin and versioned.
operator_consequence: Developers should avoid treating any agent's current provider or integration list as stable architecture.

title: Persistent agent state is becoming a product surface.
summary: Agent tools are making goals, memory, sessions, and skill maintenance more durable and visible. The useful question is which agent-side state shaped a serious run.
operator_consequence: Developers need to know which goals, memory patches, recaps, sessions, and skill maintenance loops shaped a serious run.

title: The agent interface is becoming a visible computer.
summary: Browser, desktop, file, sandbox, screenshot, and artifact surfaces are moving from nice-to-have UI into the practical interface for serious agent work.
operator_consequence: Developers increasingly need browser, desktop, file, runtime, sandbox, and artifact surfaces they can inspect.

title: Permissions, secrets, and sandboxes are moving into the foreground.
summary: The harness must now show real authority state: what the agent could read, change, execute, install, send, or leak.
operator_consequence: Developers need visible trust state: what can be read, what can be changed, which credentials are exposed, and where execution happens.

title: Accessibility is a frontier capability, not marketing polish.
summary: The most teachable work is often setup recovery, visible progress, chat/voice surfaces, OAuth clarity, readable UI, and fewer dead ends.
operator_consequence: Everyday adoption depends on whether real people can start, understand, recover, and control the agent without learning a private ontology.

title: Agent systems are growing control planes.
summary: Once agents coordinate across tasks, runtimes, gateways, and integrations, operators need liveness, cost, role, session, and recovery controls.
operator_consequence: Developers and teams need a joined operating view when agents coordinate across tasks and machines.

title: Integrations are volatile; the operating loop has to be durable.
summary: Provider lists, plugin systems, transports, model profiles, and app-server surfaces will keep changing. That is a reason to use the frontier through a durable loop.
operator_consequence: Developers should avoid treating any agent's current provider or integration list as stable architecture.
